title: path_traversal.file_read
description: Path traversal / file read proven by engine-owned contrast: the candidate (traversal) and control (benign) arms are built from one base_request slot, and a known file marker must appear in the candidate response while absent from the control.
x-notes: The engine builds the candidate and control arms by planting each payloads value into injection.location of base_request; the arms differ only in that one slot. The client never authors two independent requests.

For a query-string parameter use injection.location.kind=query (name); the param must already exist in base_request.url. For a path-segment traversal (no query parameter) use kind=url_token: place a {{slot}} token in base_request.url where the filename goes and set injection.location.name to that token; the engine substitutes each payload raw so the ../ sequence survives.
injection.payloads.candidate is the traversal value (e.g. ../../../../etc/passwd); payloads.control is a benign filename on the same endpoint (e.g. welcome.txt).

proof.expected_marker MUST be a string unique to the leaked file (e.g. "root:x:0:0:" for /etc/passwd) that the request never carries — the engine requires it present in the candidate response, absent in the control response, and absent from the candidate request it sends (reflection guard). Avoid generic strings. Prefer a planted canary file you can read back over sensitive system files.
repetitions defaults to 2; later-rep instability yields inconclusive rather than verified.
